blocks.text: With the clutch secured, set the dial gauge (A) on the clutch end plate. Pull up the clutch disc (D) until the clutch end plate (B) touches the snap ring (C) and set the dial gauge to zero at that position.

Remove your hand from the clutch disc and place the clutch compressor attachment for clearance measurement on the clutch end plate. Using a push/pull gauge, press the center of the clutch compressor attachment with a force of 39 N (4.0 kgf) (including the weight of the clutch compressor attachment) and read the scale on the dial gauge to measure the clutch clearance. Measure at least 3 places at equal intervals. Standard clearance between clutch end plate and clutch disc Forward clutch:. 0.5－0.7mm Reverse clutch 1.0－1.2mm If the clearance is out of the standard value, replace the clutch assy.
